meta.notes: Populated from WayFinder app data and verified travel references. Null fields to be enriched via Tavily web search.

language_and_money.english_proficiency: Low — limited outside tourist areas and major hotels
language_and_money.cash_vs_card: Cash dominant, especially outside Quito and Guayaquil. Cards accepted at hotels, upscale restaurants, and malls.
language_and_money.atm_availability: Widely available in cities; scarce in rural and Amazon areas. Banco Pichincha and Banco del Pacífico are most common.
language_and_money.tipping_culture: Not mandatory but appreciated. 10% service charge often included in restaurant bills.
language_and_money.tipping_etiquette: Round up taxi fares. Tip tour guides $5-10/day. Restaurants include 10% servicio but extra 5-10% appreciated for good service.

visa_requirements.us_citizen: No visa required for stays up to 90 days

entry_and_border.passport_validity_rule: Must be valid for at least 6 months beyond date of entry
entry_and_border.customs_restrictions: Strict limits on plant and animal products, especially from Galápagos. No narcotics.
entry_and_border.duty_free_allowances: 400 cigarettes or 50 cigars, 1 liter of alcohol, gifts up to $500 value

required_vaccines: Yellow Fever (if arriving from endemic country)
health.malaria_risk: Present in lowland areas below 1500m, especially Amazon basin and coastal regions
health.malaria_prophylaxis_info: Atovaquone-proguanil, doxycycline, or mefloquine recommended for Amazon travel
health.dengue_risk: Moderate — present in lowland cities and coastal areas, especially during rainy season
health.altitude_sickness_risk: High in highland regions. Quito at 2,850m. Cotopaxi base at 4,500m. Chimborazo summit at 6,263m. Acclimatize before strenuous activity.
health.food_water_safety: Tap water not safe to drink. Use bottled or purified water. Street food generally safe in busy stalls; exercise caution with raw shellfish on coast.
health.air_quality_concerns: Quito can have poor air quality due to traffic and altitude. Volcanic ash possible near active volcanoes.
health.pharmacy_access: Pharmacies (farmacias) widely available in cities. Many medications available OTC. Limited in rural areas.
health.medical_care_quality: Good in Quito and Guayaquil private hospitals. Limited in rural areas and Amazon. Evacuate for serious conditions.

risk_indicators: Crime risk moderate-high in Guayaquil and Esmeraldas
Political protests can cause road blockades
safety.crime_risk: Moderate-high in cities, especially Guayaquil (risk 5, homicide rate 42/100k) and Esmeraldas (risk 5, homicide rate 85/100k). Low in highlands and Galápagos.
safety.scam_risk: Moderate — taxi overcharging, ATM skimming, fake tour operators, distraction theft
safety.transport_safety: Intercity buses generally safe on main routes. Avoid night travel. Use registered taxis or ride-hailing apps.
safety.solo_female_travel_notes: Generally manageable with normal precautions. Avoid walking alone at night in cities. Catcalling common but rarely escalates.
safety.lgbtq_notes: Same-sex marriage legal since 2019. Generally tolerant in Quito and Cuenca. More conservative in rural areas.
safety.night_safety: Avoid walking alone at night in Guayaquil, south Quito, and Esmeraldas. Tourist areas in Quito (La Mariscal) are reasonably safe until late evening.
safety.political_stability_notes: Political protests and strikes can occur with limited notice. Road blockades possible in highlands. Monitor local news.
common_scams: Taxi overcharging — use meters or agree on price before riding
ATM skimming — use bank-attached ATMs only
Fake police asking to inspect wallet
Fake tour operators in La Mariscal
safety.police_reliability: Variable. Tourist police (Policía de Turismo) in major cities are generally helpful. Report crimes at fiscalía.

weather_and_seasonality.best_time_to_visit: June to September for highlands; December to May for coast and Galápagos; year-round for Amazon
climate_zones: Amazon basin (Oriente) — hot, humid, heavy rainfall
Galápagos — subtropical dry and warm, two distinct seasons
weather_and_seasonality.dry_season: June to September (Sierra); June to November (Galápagos garúa season)
weather_and_seasonality.rainy_season: October to May (Sierra); January to May (Costa); year-round in Amazon
weather_and_seasonality.surf_season: December to April (biggest swells); surfable year-round

major_airports: José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport (GYE) — Guayaquil
transport.public_transit_quality: Quito has BRT (Ecovía, Metrobús, Trolebús) and new Metro line. Guayaquil has Metrovía BRT. Limited elsewhere.
transport.intercity_bus_quality: Extensive and affordable bus network. Long-haul buses range from basic to semi-cama. Major terminals in every city.
transport.train_network_quality: Limited — Tren Ecuador operates scenic tourist routes (Nariz del Diablo, Quito-Otavalo). Not practical for transport.
transport.road_conditions: Main highways paved and decent. Mountain roads can be narrow and winding. Amazon roads often unpaved. Landslides in rainy season.
transport.taxi_safety_notes: Use registered taxis (yellow with orange strip in Quito) or ride-hailing apps. Agree on fare before getting in if no meter.
ferry_routes: Inter-island boats in Galápagos ($30 between main islands)
transport.border_crossing_notes: Main crossings: Tulcán-Ipiales (Colombia), Huaquillas-Aguas Verdes (Peru), Macará-La Tina (Peru). Bring passport and proof of onward travel.

budget.value_for_money: Excellent — USD economy means no exchange hassle. One of South America's most affordable countries.

connectivity.mobile_data_quality: Good 4G in cities and towns. Spotty in Amazon and remote highlands. No coverage in deep jungle.
connectivity.wifi_quality: Available in most hostels, cafes, and restaurants in tourist areas. Speeds vary (5-30 Mbps typical).
connectivity.power_reliability: Generally reliable in cities. Outages possible in rural areas and Amazon. Bring power bank for trekking.

best_areas_to_stay: La Mariscal, Quito — backpacker hub with restaurants and nightlife
Cuenca old town — colonial charm, safe, walkable
Puerto Ayora, Galápagos — main base for island exploration
accommodation.accommodation_notes: Galápagos accommodation is significantly more expensive. Book Galápagos and popular highland lodges well in advance.

local_customs: Greet with a single kiss on the right cheek (women) or handshake (men)
Family is central — Sunday family gatherings are important
Respect for elders — use 'usted' form of address
Remove shoes when entering some homes
culture.dress_expectations: Casual but conservative in highlands. Beachwear only at the beach. Cover shoulders when entering churches.
culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Catholic. Religious festivals are important cultural events. Respect processions and church services.
social_norms: Punctuality is relaxed — 15-30 minutes late is normal socially
Bargaining expected at markets but not in shops
Conversation is warm — ask about family before business
culture.greeting_style: Warm — cheek kiss for women, handshake for men. Use 'Buenos días/tardes/noches'.
culture.public_behavior_notes: PDA is acceptable in moderation. Loud behavior in public is frowned upon. Don't point with fingers — use lips or chin.
culture.photography_etiquette: Always ask permission before photographing indigenous people. Some may request a small tip. No photos in military areas.
important_holidays: Día de los Difuntos (November 2) — colada morada and guaguas de pan
legal_restrictions_tourists_often_miss: Must carry passport or copy at all times
Galápagos has strict national park rules — stay on marked trails, no touching wildlife
Drone permits required in national parks

signature_dishes: Ceviche — citrus-marinated shrimp or fish, especially on coast
Llapingachos — mashed potato patties with cheese, served with peanut sauce
Seco de pollo — chicken stew with beer and cilantro
Encebollado — tuna and yuca soup, hangover cure of Guayaquil
Fritada — slow-cooked pork with mote (hominy) and llapingachos
Cuy — roasted guinea pig, highland delicacy
Sopa de bolas de verde — green plantain dumpling soup
Fanesca — rich Easter soup with 12 grains and salt cod
food.street_food_safety: Generally safe at busy stalls. Avoid raw shellfish from unrefrigerated vendors. Stick to cooked and hot items.
food.vegetarian_friendliness: Moderate — rice, beans, and potatoes are abundant. Dedicated vegetarian restaurants in Quito and Cuenca. Many soups contain meat stock.
food.vegan_friendliness: Challenging outside major cities. Growing vegan scene in Quito.
must_try_drinks: Colada morada — purple corn and berry drink (Día de Difuntos)
Chicha de jora — fermented corn beer
Agua de guayusa — Amazonian caffeinated tea
Hot chocolate — made with real Ecuadorian cacao
regional_specialties: Costa: ceviche, encebollado, green plantain dishes
Sierra: cuy, locro de papa, fritada, llapingachos
Oriente: maito (fish wrapped in bijao leaf), chontaduro fruit
Galápagos: fresh lobster (seasonal), ceviche de canchalagua
food.drinking_water_notes: Never drink tap water. Bottled water available everywhere ($0.50-1). Verify ice is from purified water in restaurants.
food.alcohol_rules: Legal drinking age 18. Ley seca (dry law) prohibits alcohol sales during elections and some holidays.

top_national_parks: Galápagos National Park — unique endemic wildlife, marine reserves, iconic islands
Cotopaxi National Park — snow-capped volcano (5,897m), páramo hiking
El Cajas National Park — 230+ alpine lakes, páramo trails, near Cuenca
Machalilla National Park — coastal dry forest, whale watching, Los Frailes beach
Yasuní National Park — most biodiverse place on Earth, Amazon rainforest
Podocarpus National Park — cloud forest, 600+ bird species

top_day_hikes.name: Rucu Pichincha
top_day_hikes.description: Hike above Quito via TelefériQo cable car to the summit of Rucu Pichincha volcano at 4,696m. Stunning views of the city and surrounding volcanoes.
top_day_hikes.notes: Take TelefériQo up to 4,100m then hike. Altitude can be challenging — acclimatize first. Weather changes fast.

top_day_hikes.name: Pasochoa Wildlife Refuge
top_day_hikes.description: Extinct volcano crater with native Andean forest. Excellent birdwatching. One of few remaining intact highland forests near Quito.
top_day_hikes.notes: Entry fee required. Less crowded than Cotopaxi. Good acclimatization hike.

top_day_hikes.name: Mindo Cloud Forest Trails
top_day_hikes.description: Network of trails through cloud forest. World-class birdwatching — Cock-of-the-rock, Toucan Barbet, glass frogs. Waterfalls and butterfly gardens.
top_day_hikes.notes: Can be combined with zip-lining and chocolate tours. Expect rain year-round. 2 hours from Quito.

top_day_hikes.name: El Cajas National Park Circuit
top_day_hikes.description: High-altitude páramo hiking among 230+ glacial lakes. Spectacled bear and Andean condor habitat. Polylepis forest.
top_day_hikes.notes: 30 minutes from Cuenca. Weather extremely changeable — bring layers. Register at park entrance.

top_day_hikes.name: Sierra Negra Volcano — Galápagos
top_day_hikes.description: Hike to the rim of one of the world's largest volcanic calderas. Giant tortoises, marine iguanas, and lava fields.
top_day_hikes.notes: Must go with licensed guide. $100 Galápagos National Park fee. Last erupted 2018.

top_day_hikes.name: Tungurahua Volcano Viewpoint
top_day_hikes.description: Trek to viewpoints of the active Tungurahua volcano from Baños. Páramo landscapes with hummingbirds and condors.
top_day_hikes.notes: Check volcanic activity status before hiking. Multiple routes of varying difficulty from Baños.

multi_day_treks.name: Quilotoa Loop
multi_day_treks.description: Classic highland trek through indigenous villages to the stunning turquoise Quilotoa crater lake at 3,914m. Andean condors and páramo landscapes.
multi_day_treks.notes: Can be shortened to 2 days. Basic guesthouses in villages along route. Carry cash — no ATMs on route.

multi_day_treks.name: Cotopaxi Volcano Trek
multi_day_treks.description: Trek to the José Ribas refuge at 4,800m on one of the world's highest active volcanoes. Summit attempt requires mountaineering experience and guide.
multi_day_treks.notes: Summit requires crampons, ice axe, and licensed guide. Altitude acclimatization essential. Park entry fee $15.

multi_day_treks.name: Chimborazo
multi_day_treks.description: Ecuador's highest peak and the point on Earth's surface farthest from the center. Refuge hike is moderate; summit requires mountaineering skills.
multi_day_treks.notes: Two refuges: first at 4,800m (moderate hike), second at 5,000m. Summit is technical. Vicuñas visible on lower slopes.

multi_day_treks.name: Illiniza Norte
multi_day_treks.description: Non-technical summit of Illiniza Norte (5,126m) with views of Cotopaxi and the Avenue of Volcanoes. Good preparation for higher peaks.
multi_day_treks.notes: Refuge available at 4,650m. Illiniza Sur (5,248m) requires technical climbing. Scrambling near summit — not a walk-up.

multi_day_treks.name: Antisana
multi_day_treks.description: Remote volcanic peak with glaciers. Excellent wildlife — Andean condors are frequently spotted. Less crowded than Cotopaxi.
multi_day_treks.notes: Access road can be rough. Condor viewing possible without summiting. Ecological reserve entry required.

multi_day_treks.name: Podocarpus National Park Trek
multi_day_treks.description: Cloud forest and páramo trails with extraordinary bird diversity (600+ species). Spectacled bears and mountain tapir habitat.
multi_day_treks.notes: Two main entrances: Cajanuma (high altitude) and Bombuscaro (lowland). The Royal Sunangel hummingbird is endemic to this area.

volcanoes: Chimborazo (6,263m) — highest peak, farthest point from Earth's center
Reventador (3,562m) — one of the most active in Ecuador
Sangay (5,230m) — remote, UNESCO World Heritage, constantly active
Cayambe (5,790m) — highest point on the equator with a glacier
waterfalls: Pailón del Diablo — Baños, dramatic 80m waterfall in gorge
San Rafael Falls — highest waterfall in Ecuador (150m, reduced flow since 2020)
Mindo waterfalls — multiple falls accessible by tarabita (cable car)
hot_springs: Termas de Papallacta — highland hot springs at 3,300m, near Quito
Baños de Agua Santa — thermal baths at base of Tungurahua
lakes: Cuicocha — crater lake with islands, Imbabura
El Cajas lakes — 230+ glacial lakes in páramo
Lake San Pablo — largest lake in Ecuador, Otavalo area
rivers: Napo River — major Amazon tributary, wildlife viewing
Blanco River — whitewater rafting near Baños
Pastaza River — canyon views and rafting from Baños
birdwatching_hotspots: Mindo Cloud Forest — 500+ species, Cock-of-the-rock leks
Podocarpus NP — 600+ species, Royal Sunangel endemic
Yasuní NP — 600+ species including harpy eagle
El Cajas NP — Andean species, violet-throated metaltail
stargazing_spots: Cotopaxi National Park — high altitude, minimal light pollution
El Cajas NP — 4,000m+ elevation, clear skies
scenic_drives: Avenue of the Volcanoes — Pan-American Highway through Andean valley flanked by volcanoes
Ruta del Spondylus — coastal highway from Esmeraldas to Santa Elena
Baños to Puyo — Ruta de las Cascadas waterfall route

surf_spots.name: Montañita
surf_spots.description: Ecuador's most famous surf town. Consistent right point break with powerful waves. International competitions held here. Vibrant nightlife.
surf_spots.notes: Crowded during holidays. Board rentals and lessons readily available. Party town atmosphere.

surf_spots.name: Ayampe
surf_spots.description: Mellow beach break in a quiet fishing village. Great for learning and intermediate surfers. Eco-lodges and yoga retreats.
surf_spots.notes: Much quieter than Montañita. Growing surf scene. Good for those wanting to escape the party.

surf_spots.name: Canoa
surf_spots.description: Long wide beach with gentle waves ideal for beginners. Laid-back town rebuilding after 2016 earthquake. Paragliding also popular.
surf_spots.notes: Rebuilding infrastructure. Budget-friendly. Good for longboarding.

surf_spots.name: Los Frailes
surf_spots.description: Beautiful crescent beach inside Machalilla National Park. Less consistent surf but stunning setting. Whale watching in season.
surf_spots.notes: National park entry fee. Limited facilities. Combine with Isla de la Plata ('Poor Man's Galápagos').

surf_spots.name: Mompiche
surf_spots.description: Powerful left point break in a remote Afro-Ecuadorian fishing village. Long rides possible. One of Ecuador's best waves when working.
surf_spots.notes: Remote — limited infrastructure. Check safety situation in Esmeraldas province. Mosquitoes.

surf_spots.name: San Lorenzo (Manta area)
surf_spots.description: Reef and point breaks near the port city of Manta. More consistent than many spots. Local surf community.
surf_spots.notes: Near Manta with good infrastructure. Multiple breaks in the area. Can be hollow and powerful.

surf_spots.name: Salinas
surf_spots.description: Popular resort town with several surf breaks. Chipipe beach good for beginners. Punta Carnero has bigger waves nearby.
surf_spots.notes: Most touristy beach town in Ecuador. Better infrastructure than other surf spots. Crowded on weekends.

surf_spots.name: Puerto Cayo
surf_spots.description: Small fishing town with uncrowded beach breaks. Whale watching from June to September. Growing surf scene.
surf_spots.notes: Quiet and off the beaten path. Limited services. Good base for exploring Machalilla NP.

wildlife_zones.name: Galápagos Islands
wildlife_zones.notes: UNESCO World Heritage. $100 park fee + $20 transit card. Must visit with licensed naturalist guide. Book well in advance.

wildlife_zones.name: Yasuní National Park
wildlife_zones.notes: Most biodiverse place on Earth per hectare. Access via Coca (Puerto Francisco de Orellana). Multi-day lodge stays. Napo Wildlife Center is top option.

wildlife_zones.name: Mindo-Nambillo Cloud Forest
wildlife_zones.notes: 500+ bird species. 2 hours from Quito. Multiple lodges with hummingbird feeders. Cock-of-the-rock lek viewing at dawn.

wildlife_zones.name: Cayambe-Coca Ecological Reserve
wildlife_zones.notes: Protects páramo to cloud forest. Includes Papallacta hot springs area. Condor sightings at Antisana. Less visited than Cotopaxi.

wildlife_zones.name: Cuyabeno Wildlife Reserve
wildlife_zones.notes: Flooded forest ecosystem. Canoe-based exploration. More affordable than Yasuní. 4-5 day tours from Lago Agrio. Check security in Sucumbíos.

sports_and_activities.name: Surfing
sports_and_activities.notes: Biggest swells December-April. Board rentals $10-15/day. Lessons $20-30.

sports_and_activities.name: Mountaineering
sports_and_activities.notes: Avenue of the Volcanoes offers multiple 5,000m+ peaks. Licensed guide required for most summits. Acclimatization essential.

sports_and_activities.name: Mountain Biking
sports_and_activities.notes: Cotopaxi descent from 4,500m is iconic. Baños has multiple downhill routes. Bike rentals $10-20/day.

sports_and_activities.name: Kayaking
sports_and_activities.notes: Sea kayaking with sea lions in Galápagos. River kayaking in Amazon tributaries. Whitewater kayaking near Baños.

sports_and_activities.name: Birdwatching
sports_and_activities.notes: Ecuador has the world's highest bird diversity per unit area — 1,600+ species. Top destination globally for birding.

sports_and_activities.name: Whitewater Rafting
sports_and_activities.notes: Class III-IV rapids on Toachi and Blanco. Day trips from Quito or Baños. $50-80 for full-day trips.

sports_and_activities.name: Paragliding
sports_and_activities.notes: Tandem flights available in Baños and Canoa. $30-50 for tandem flights. Thermals good in dry season.

sample_itineraries.one_week: Quito (2 days) → Otavalo market (1 day) → Cotopaxi (1 day) → Baños (2 days) → Quito (1 day)
sample_itineraries.two_week: Quito (2 days) → Mindo (2 days) → Quilotoa Loop (3 days) → Baños (2 days) → Cuenca (2 days) → Guayaquil (1 day) → Montañita (2 days)
sample_itineraries.classic_route: Quito → Avenue of Volcanoes → Baños → Cuenca → Coast → Galápagos
sample_itineraries.offbeat_route: Quito → Mindo → Mompiche surf → Cuyabeno Amazon → Tena → Baños

practical_info.arrival_tips: Quito airport (UIO) is 30-45 min from city center. Arrange airport transfer in advance or use authorized taxi. Register at STEP before travel.
practical_info.airport_scam_notes: Only use authorized airport taxis from the official taxi counter. Unofficial taxis may overcharge or worse.
practical_info.local_sim_where_to_buy: Claro or Movistar SIM cards available at airport kiosks and city shops. Bring passport. $5-10 for data plan.
practical_info.money_exchange_notes: Ecuador uses USD — no exchange needed for US travelers. Carry small bills ($1, $5, $10) as change is scarce. $50 and $100 bills often refused.

laws.drug_laws_strictness: Strict — possession of small amounts decriminalized but amounts above threshold carry severe penalties. Do not carry drugs.
laws.alcohol_laws: Legal age 18. Ley seca during elections and some holidays — alcohol sales prohibited. Public drunkenness can result in fines.
laws.drone_rules: Registration required with DGAC. Prohibited in Galápagos NP and over populated areas without permit. Altitude limit 120m.
laws.wild_camping_rules: Generally permitted in rural areas with landowner permission. Not allowed in national parks without specific camping zones.
laws.protected_area_rules: National park fees apply. Galápagos has strict rules — stay on trails, no touching wildlife, certified guide required.
laws.lgbtq_legal_status: Same-sex marriage legal since June 2019. Anti-discrimination protections exist.
laws.marijuana_legality: Small amounts (up to 10g) decriminalized. Sale and cultivation illegal.
laws.prescription_medication_rules: Bring prescriptions in original packaging with doctor's letter. Controlled substances may require additional documentation.

family_travel.car_seat_rules: Required for children under 12 in front seat. Not always enforced. Bring your own — rentals unreliable.
family_activities: Galápagos wildlife watching — snorkeling with sea lions, tortoise breeding centers
Mindo cloud forest — chocolate tours, zip-lining, butterfly gardens
Mitad del Mundo (equator monument) — interactive science museum
Otavalo market — colorful indigenous crafts market

accessibility.wheelchair_accessibility: Limited — Quito and Cuenca have some accessible infrastructure but uneven sidewalks common. Galápagos boats vary. Most rural areas inaccessible.
accessibility.accessible_transport: Quito Metro has accessibility features. Most buses are not wheelchair accessible. Private transfers recommended.
accessible_attractions: Some Galápagos visitor sites with boardwalks
